title: Poa durifolia
taxonomy: Poa durifolia is classified within the family Poaceae, commonly known as the grass family. This plant belongs to the tribe Poeae and subfamily Pooideae. Poa durifolia is further classified as a species within the genus Poa, which consists of many species of grasses. The genus Poa is divided into several sections, with Poa durifolia being part of the section Stenantha. Poa durifolia is native to the mountainous regions of New Zealand, where it grows in a variety of habitats.

intro: Poa durifolia, also known as rough blue grass, is a perennial grass native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Western Asia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties that may aid in the treatment of various conditions, such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Poa durifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs to enhance their effects.
